description: Mixarr nightly pipeline, enrichment coverage, engines and provider HTTP visibility, built on the metrics exposed by the /metrics endpoint (METRICS_PORT). Filter by Prometheus job/instance using the variables at the top.

panels.title: Tracks indexed
panels.description: Total tracks Mixarr has cached from Plex (refreshed on each /metrics scrape, throttled to once every 15s).

panels.title: Spotify rate-limit backoff
panels.description: Seconds remaining on the persisted Spotify 429 backoff. 0 means we're free to call Spotify; anything > 0 means Spotify is currently shut off and the timer is counting down.

panels.title: Enrichment coverage (% of library)
panels.description: For each enrichment kind, what percentage of indexed tracks have a successful result. 'not_found' marker rows are excluded from the numerator but kept in the denominator (mixarr_tracks_total).

panels.title: Enriched track count over time
panels.description: Absolute count of tracks per enrichment kind and outcome. 'success' = real data was returned, 'not_found' = a marker row was written so we don't ask again.

panels.title: Nightly pipeline runs
panels.description: Count of pipeline invocations broken down by result. Stacked bars; each bucket width is the dashboard interval. 'skipped' usually means a previous run was still in flight.

panels.title: Pipeline duration (avg per window)
panels.description: Average wall-clock duration of recent pipeline runs. Computed as rate(_sum) / rate(_count) so sparse nightly runs still produce a meaningful trend line.

panels.title: Plex library syncs
panels.description: Library sync invocations from Plex, by result.

panels.title: Tracks processed per second (by engine)
panels.description: rate() of mixarr_track_attempts_total grouped by engine. Shows how fast each enrichment engine (popularity / audio_feature / bpm / tags) is chewing through work. Note: the bpm engine includes a Deezer lookup plus, on a miss, a local ffmpeg sample + aubio tempo analysis, so its per-track rate is naturally much lower than the others.

panels.title: Engine outcomes (stacked, all engines)
panels.description: Per-second outcome breakdown across all engines. A growing red/orange band means provider problems are leaking through to the engines.

panels.title: Engine work share (selected range)
panels.description: Proportion of all track attempts that went to each engine over the selected time range. Useful for seeing if one engine is monopolizing the work.

panels.title: Engine success rate (selected range)
panels.description: success / total per engine over the selected time range. 'not_found' counts as a non-success here so a low number can mean either real failures or genuine misses.

targets.expr: 100 * sum by (engine) (increase(mixarr_track_attempts_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", result="success"}[$__range])) / clamp_min(sum by (engine) (increase(mixarr_track_attempts_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[$__range])), 1)

panels.title: Engine batch size (p50 / p95)
panels.description: Size of each batch the engine pulls off its work queue. Drops to 0 when the engine has fully drained.

panels.title: Per-track processing duration (heatmap, all engines)
panels.description: Distribution of per-track processing time. Brighter cells = more tracks landed in that latency bucket. A drift toward higher buckets = engines slowing down.

panels.title: Per-track processing duration (p50 / p95 / p99 by engine)
Provider call share (selected range)
panels.description: Where the API budget is going. Sized by total call count over the selected time range.

targets.expr: 100 * sum by (provider) (increase(mixarr_provider_requests_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", result="success"}[$__range])) / clamp_min(sum by (provider) (increase(mixarr_provider_requests_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[$__range])), 1)

panels.title: Spotify rate-limit backoff over time
panels.description: How long the persisted Spotify backoff has had remaining at each point in time. Spikes correspond to 429 responses; the line then ticks down toward 0 as the backoff expires.

panels.title: Last.fm autocorrections / second
panels.description: Rate of Last.fm responses where the server rewrote the artist, track, or both before lookup. Only ticks above zero when LASTFM_AUTOCORRECT=1 in the environment; a flat-zero line means the feature is disabled or your library's metadata already matches Last.fm's canonical names. Cross-reference '[Last.fm] Autocorrected ...' log lines in Graylog to inspect the actual rewrites.

panels.title: Autocorrect share of all Last.fm calls (selected range)
panels.description: Of every Last.fm request over the selected time range, what fraction came back with an autocorrected artist and/or track. 0% with LASTFM_AUTOCORRECT=0 (the default) is expected. With autocorrect enabled, a moderate value (~10-30%) typically just means Plex metadata isn't perfectly canonicalized. A very high % may indicate autocorrect is being too aggressive on obscure tracks - check Graylog for individual rewrites.

panels.title: Event loop lag (p99)
panels.description: Spikes here while logs go quiet usually mean Mixarr is starved on CPU rather than blocked on a hung HTTP call.

title: Mixarr
